rolloutSemantics.enabled: Shown by default under things to improve or cost opportunities.
rolloutSemantics.diagnostic: Shown by default under things to watch, with its claim limit.
rolloutSemantics.shadow: Measured only by local dogfood tooling; never rendered or sent as telemetry.
rolloutSemantics.disabled: Preserved in the catalog but not executed.

privacy.renderedEvidence: Counts, turn indices, fixed enums, and sanitized tool names only.

rollout.reason: Existing detector after its missing-input and wording defects are fixed.

repeated-identical-attempt.title: Same action repeated without a change
repeated-identical-attempt.description: The same tool ran at least three times in a row with identical recorded input.
repeated-identical-attempt.whyItMatters: Repeating an unchanged action can use more time and tokens without trying a new approach.
repeated-identical-attempt.recommendation: After two identical attempts, inspect the result and change the input or approach before trying again.
repeated-identical-attempt.claimLimit: This proves repetition, not that the repeated action was unnecessary or failed.

rollout.reason: Explicit error status makes the prevention advice stronger than the generic repeated-attempt pattern.

repeated-identical-error.title: The same failed action was tried again unchanged
repeated-identical-error.description: The same tool and recorded input ended with an error twice within ten tool actions, with no recorded write or successful check between.
repeated-identical-error.whyItMatters: The second failed attempt did not follow a recorded workspace change or successful check.
repeated-identical-error.recommendation: After the same action fails twice without recorded progress, inspect the error and change the input or approach, or record the blocker before trying again.
repeated-identical-error.claimLimit: Expected negative probes can fail on purpose; this does not prove the attempt was irrational.

rollout.reason: Actionable, but expected negative tests and exploratory probes need a clear caveat.

consecutive-tool-errors.title: Several actions failed in a row
consecutive-tool-errors.description: At least three consecutive recorded tool calls ended with an error status.
consecutive-tool-errors.whyItMatters: A chain of errors is a useful point to pause before more retries compound the problem.
consecutive-tool-errors.recommendation: After two consecutive tool errors, pause, inspect the recorded failures, and change one variable before continuing.
consecutive-tool-errors.claimLimit: The errors may be deliberate probes and may not share a cause.

rollout.reason: In the local audit, 525 of 629 qualifying roots later reached a write or validation; the rule mostly described normal research rather than a problem.

search-streak-without-change-or-check.title: Long search streak before the next concrete step
search-streak-without-change-or-check.description: Ten searches or file reads occurred in a row without a recorded write or validation command.
search-streak-without-change-or-check.whyItMatters: Long exploration streaks are a useful checkpoint because a session can keep gathering context without testing a hypothesis.
search-streak-without-change-or-check.recommendation: After ten searches or reads without a change or check, summarize what is known and choose one concrete next step before searching more.
search-streak-without-change-or-check.claimLimit: This does not prove that any search or read was unnecessary.

rollout.reason: Promotion needs reliable resets for writes, compactions, successful state changes, and external-data refreshes across every source.

repeated-search-query.title: The same search was repeated
repeated-search-query.description: The same normalized search query appeared at least twice within ten actions without an intervening write or compaction.
repeated-search-query.whyItMatters: Repeating the same search without changing the workspace is unlikely to reveal a different result.
repeated-search-query.recommendation: Reuse the prior result or change the query, scope, or hypothesis before searching again.
repeated-search-query.claimLimit: External files or search indexes could have changed without a recorded workspace write.

rollout.reason: Already extracted as a low-confidence receipt diagnostic; range changes and external mutations remain legitimate explanations.

same-file-reread-without-recorded-change.title: The same file was read repeatedly without a recorded change
same-file-reread-without-recorded-change.description: The same canonical file path was read at least three times within ten actions without a recorded write to that file.
same-file-reread-without-recorded-change.whyItMatters: Repeated full reads can refill context with information that may already be available.
same-file-reread-without-recorded-change.recommendation: Before reading the same file again, record what fresh information is needed; prefer a narrower range when only one section matters.
same-file-reread-without-recorded-change.claimLimit: Different ranges, deliberate re-grounding, and unrecorded external changes can make a reread useful.

rollout.reason: Large local coverage gain, but source-write and validation classifiers need adversarial false-positive calibration first.

last-change-not-checked.title: The final code change had no later recorded check
last-change-not-checked.description: A recorded source-code write occurred and no successful recognized validation command was recorded after the final source write.
last-change-not-checked.whyItMatters: The trace cannot show that the final version of the code passed a relevant check.
last-change-not-checked.recommendation: After the last code change, run the narrowest relevant check; if no check applies, record that reason explicitly.
last-change-not-checked.claimLimit: A useful check may be unrecognized, and some code-like changes may not require an executable test.

rollout.reason: Requires a stable validation-key classifier and explicit command status across sources.

last-check-still-failing.title: The last recorded check was still failing
last-check-still-failing.description: The last recognized validation for a check key ended with an error and no later passing run of that key was recorded.
last-check-still-failing.whyItMatters: The recorded evidence ends with a known failing check.
last-check-still-failing.recommendation: Fix the failure and rerun the check, run a narrower relevant check, or record the blocker before ending the session.
last-check-still-failing.claimLimit: A failing check can be an intentional red-phase test or an accepted blocker.

rollout.reason: A running status can mean an active capture, a truncated transcript, or an adapter pairing gap; no cross-source terminal-session proof exists yet.

unresolved-tool-call.title: An action has no recorded completion
unresolved-tool-call.description: One or more tool calls still have a recorded running status at the end of the transcript.
unresolved-tool-call.whyItMatters: The trace does not contain a final result for those actions.
unresolved-tool-call.recommendation: Before ending, verify or cancel every action that lacks a recorded terminal result.
unresolved-tool-call.claimLimit: The transcript may have been captured while work was still running; do not call the action abandoned.

rollout.reason: Prose plans and planner-tool availability differ by agent, and many bounded tasks legitimately need no separate structured plan.

many-writes-without-recorded-plan.title: Many changes were made without a recorded plan
many-writes-without-recorded-plan.description: At least five file writes occurred without a preceding structured plan or TODO tool call when that tool surface was available.
many-writes-without-recorded-plan.whyItMatters: A short plan can keep a multi-change task from losing requirements or verification steps.
many-writes-without-recorded-plan.recommendation: Before a task that needs several changes, record a short ordered plan and keep it current as work changes.
many-writes-without-recorded-plan.claimLimit: The task may be simple enough that a separate recorded plan would add no value.

rollout.reason: Cross-source write failure and reversal effects are not normalized reliably enough.

failed-read-write-oscillation.title: The session cycled around the same failed edit
failed-read-write-oscillation.description: For one file, at least two read-write-read cycles contain a middle write explicitly labeled failed or reverted.
failed-read-write-oscillation.whyItMatters: Repeatedly returning to the same failed edit is a strong sign that the current approach needs a new hypothesis.
failed-read-write-oscillation.recommendation: After a failed edit cycle repeats, stop editing, restate the failure evidence, and choose a different change before continuing.
failed-read-write-oscillation.claimLimit: No claim is emitted while failed and reverted write effects are incomplete.

rollout.reason: Existing detector with its causal wording and impact semantics corrected.

context-refill-cluster.title: Context returned to a previous peak after nearby compactions
context-refill-cluster.description: At least two nearby compactions were each followed within five turns by prompt load reaching at least 80% of the earlier peak.
context-refill-cluster.whyItMatters: Repeatedly returning to a large prompt footprint can make one session expensive and harder to steer.
context-refill-cluster.recommendation: Split distinct tasks into separate sessions and keep only current-task evidence when context is compacted.
context-refill-cluster.claimLimit: The trace does not prove that the refilled context was old, unnecessary, or unrelated to new work.

rollout.reason: Existing deterministic same-token repricing opportunity after its content claims and source-wide vendor gate are corrected.

short-tool-free-turn-cost.title: Short tool-free turns used a higher-priced tier
short-tool-free-turn-cost.description: Tool-free turns with at most 120 recorded output tokens used directly priced models that have a lower-priced current row from the same provider.
short-tool-free-turn-cost.whyItMatters: The same recorded token volume has a lower list-price arithmetic result, although task quality is unknown.
short-tool-free-turn-cost.recommendation: Keep short tool-free replies minimal; where routing is already available, consider the lower-cost tier for this narrow class of turn.
short-tool-free-turn-cost.claimLimit: A short answer can still require difficult reasoning; this never claims another model would complete the task.

rollout.reason: Adapters and source stores truncate or omit output differently, so recorded character counts are not comparable enough even for shadow promotion work.

large-tool-output.title: A tool returned a very large recorded output
large-tool-output.description: A single tool result crossed a frozen recorded-size threshold in a source that preserves output completeness.
large-tool-output.whyItMatters: Large outputs can fill context with material that is not needed for the next decision.
large-tool-output.recommendation: Narrow the command, file range, or result limit so the next run returns only the evidence needed.
large-tool-output.claimLimit: Recorded characters are not tokens, and a large output can be necessary.

rollout.reason: External evidence shows this mainly reflects the agent scaffold, not a per-session failure.

shell-over-structured-tool.title: A shell command duplicated an available structured tool
shell-over-structured-tool.description: A shell read or search command was used while an equivalent structured tool was exposed.
shell-over-structured-tool.whyItMatters: Structured tools can provide more bounded and consistently recorded evidence.
shell-over-structured-tool.recommendation: Prefer the available structured read or search tool when it can express the same bounded request.
shell-over-structured-tool.claimLimit: No finding is emitted because shell use can be the correct scaffold convention.

rollout.reason: The normalized model intentionally omits assistant prose, and a fixed text regex would still not prove the claim false.

unsupported-completion-claim.title: Completion was stated without a later recorded check
unsupported-completion-claim.description: Final assistant text matches a completion phrase and no successful recognized validation follows the last source write.
unsupported-completion-claim.whyItMatters: Completion language is not evidence that the final code was checked.
unsupported-completion-claim.recommendation: Tie completion statements to a recorded passing check or state clearly that verification was not run.
unsupported-completion-claim.claimLimit: No assistant text is ingested or rendered for this detector.

rollout.reason: Published versions require model-generated semantic phase labels and would violate deterministic zero-model-call operation.

semantic-phase-oscillation.title: The session repeatedly switched between the same work phases
semantic-phase-oscillation.description: The same two semantic phases alternate at least three times within six actions.
semantic-phase-oscillation.whyItMatters: Repeated switching can indicate that the current hypothesis is not converging.
semantic-phase-oscillation.recommendation: Pause after repeated phase switches, state one hypothesis and its decisive check, then follow that path before switching again.
semantic-phase-oscillation.claimLimit: Never run this detector in the deterministic product path.

rollout.reason: Published versions require model-generated semantic labels and later-intent judgments.

semantic-fruitless-exploration.title: Exploration did not connect to later implementation
semantic-fruitless-exploration.description: At least four of five semantically labeled code reads never appear in a later implementation step.
semantic-fruitless-exploration.whyItMatters: Exploration that never informs a change can consume context without narrowing the task.
semantic-fruitless-exploration.recommendation: After several exploratory reads, summarize which evidence changes the implementation plan and drop paths that do not.
semantic-fruitless-exploration.claimLimit: Never run this detector in the deterministic product path.

rollout.reason: A single local session has no trustworthy gold or successful reference scope.

reference-scope-drift.title: Work moved outside a reference change scope
reference-scope-drift.description: Recorded writes target files outside an explicit reference or gold change set.
reference-scope-drift.whyItMatters: Unexpected scope expansion is a useful review checkpoint when a valid reference exists.
reference-scope-drift.recommendation: Before changing files outside the agreed scope, record why the expansion is necessary and verify the added surface.
reference-scope-drift.claimLimit: Do not infer task scope from a single session or live repository state.

rollout.reason: A quick rewrite can be normal; published disambiguation requires a reference run.

reference-relative-rapid-rewrite.title: A change was quickly overwritten or reversed
reference-relative-rapid-rewrite.description: A file write is overwritten or reversed within three actions and a valid reference run does not show the same rewrite.
reference-relative-rapid-rewrite.whyItMatters: Quick reversals can identify unstable edits that deserve a more explicit hypothesis.
reference-relative-rapid-rewrite.recommendation: Before rewriting the same change again, state what the previous edit disproved and run a focused check.
reference-relative-rapid-rewrite.claimLimit: No single-session finding is emitted because rapid correction can be healthy work.

rollout.reason: Structured task state is not normalized and sampled snapshots can be stale after later activity.

open-task-at-end.title: Recorded tasks were still open at the end
open-task-at-end.description: The latest trustworthy structured task snapshot contains pending or in-progress items at session end.
open-task-at-end.whyItMatters: Open tracked work can be forgotten when the session is treated as complete.
open-task-at-end.recommendation: Before ending, complete each open task or record its blocker and next concrete action.
open-task-at-end.claimLimit: Never treat a stale task snapshot as current state.

rollout.reason: Explicit interruption lifecycle events are not normalized consistently across sources.

interrupted-work.title: An interrupted operation had no recorded resumption
interrupted-work.description: An explicit interrupted or aborted event has no later matching resumed completion.
interrupted-work.whyItMatters: Interrupted work can leave a partial change or missing result behind.
interrupted-work.recommendation: After an interruption, resume the operation or record the blocker and the next safe action.
interrupted-work.claimLimit: A truncated transcript may omit a completion that occurred later.

rollout.reason: Child lifecycle and parent-delivery evidence are not normalized across sources.

subagent-delivery-gap.title: A child result was not recorded as delivered to its parent
subagent-delivery-gap.description: A child run records completion but the parent trace has no matching delivery event.
subagent-delivery-gap.whyItMatters: Completed delegated work can be lost if its result never reaches the parent session.
subagent-delivery-gap.recommendation: Before ending, confirm that every completed child result was received or record which delivery failed.
subagent-delivery-gap.claimLimit: Do not infer delivery failure from child existence alone.
